ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING , REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (Trustee) Vera Sprinkel. Ron Jaeger (via court call) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is the 9th interim report and accounting, that it has been amended, that objections have been filed, that there is a request for distribution and argument regarding alleged obligations of Denny and asked if distribution was in the works. Ms. Ng reported that distribution is being worked on and that they filed the 9th report prior to Ms. Jaeger's death. Mr. Jaeger presented his argument regarding the renegotiated loan, the lack of disclosure as to what notes were renegotiated and in response to the court stating that this issue was before the court in 2008 stated that it was never resolved. Mr. Jaeger states that there is issue with an accounting discrepancy of $200,000.00 missing from what was initially reported by Debra Dolch, that there is a bank account not being put into the reports, that Debra Dolch indicated that Denny had undue influence over Ms. Jaeger and was going to investigate but the investigation did not take place and that APS was called in when Ms. Jaeger cut two checks for Denny without promissory notes. Mr. Jaeger talked about distribution to the heirs, that the church is to get their distribution first, followed by $50,000.00 each to himself and Michael Jaeger, that there should be no distribution to Denny and asked about his mothers ashes, an accounting of personal effects and the life insurance policy. Ms. Ng reported that Ms. Sprinkle is a longtime fiduciary, that there is no motivation for her to represent Denny, that the bank account in question could be a joint account that is not in the trust, that the $185,000.00 promissory note is the only one that is known about, that the inventory of the disputed $200,000.00 was addressed in 2008, that it was Ms. Jaeger's counsel who re-negotiated the note, that Ms. Jaeger was represented by counsel when the accusations of undue influence were made, that assets have been liquidated and requested 60 days to file the final accounting. Ms. Ng further stated that she is in receipt of a letter from Mr. Holsman's office dated 10/11/07 but that it is not on file with the court. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Continued To Jul-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Holsman for Emily Jaeger. Lillian Ng for and with Vera Sprinkel.

Mr. Hollsman stated that he has not seen Ms. Jaeger in over one year, that he spoke with her in late February/March, that Ms. Jaeger always says that everything is fine, that he thinks she is being well taken care of and that He and the Trustee agreed to ask Denny (Son) bring Ms. Jaeger here so that they could meet. Ms. Sprinkel stated that she receives reports from Ms. Jeager's Son, that the Family situation has been harmonized, that she is the named payee for Social Security, that she deals with the IRA, that she thinks Denny is willing to take over the responsibility of the assets and that he is taking more on. Ms. Ng addressed the budget, care costs, stated that she could not obtain a letter from the Doctor regarding the level care needed, that she received a letter from the Care Home indicating that Ms. Jaeger requires 24/7 care and explained the purpose of the car. The Court expressed concern about Ms. Jaeger's well being and management of the Trust. The matter was continued to July 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Sprinkle to do a visit with Ms. Jaeger. Ms. Sprinkle is to file a declaration by July 13, 2011. Mr. Holsman will go with Ms. Sprinkel free of charge. The Examiner will fax notes indicating whether an appearance is required or whether the matter is under submission on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of The Former Trustee'S Final Account
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of The Former Trustee'S Final Account
Off Calendar Oct-06-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Vera Sprinkel.

The Court inquired whether there were any objections to Vera Sprinkel being reappointed. Ms. Ng stated that she had spoken with Mr. Holsman and that he had no objection, that she had not spoken with Mr. Patterson, and that she assumed by him not being present today that there was no problem with the reappointment. The Court inquired whether any further notice would be required and stated that it was little concerned with reappointing Ms. Sprinkel. Ms. Ng stated that there are two other sons, that there wasn't a notice of hearing because this status hearing was set as part of her accounting order, and that it was intended to go off calendar but then it developed into this big problem with the bonding company. The Court stated that possibly the thing to do would be for Ms. Ng to bring an ex-parte petition to reappoint Ms. Sprinkel so that everyone receives notice. Ms. Ng stated that she could file the ex-parte in about a week and that if it goes through then there won't be another appearance. The matter was continued to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of The Former Trustee'S Final Account
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Holsman for Emily Jaeger. Lillian Ng for Vera Sprinkle (Trustee).

Mr. Holsman stated that the Bonding company was not willing to bond Mr. Jaeger for the outstanding note of $185,000.00 but that they would bond for the other assets, that Mrs. Jaeger was very happy living with the Son, that the brother Michael Jaeger was okay with Denny being the Trustee but that he was not comfortable with it because Denny has too much going on with runnning the business, and that he supports Ms. Sprinkle continuing to serve as Trustee. Mr. Holsman also spoke of Mr. Patterson filing an ex parte petition regarding the bond issue. Ms. Ng spoke of the difficulties with tracking/handling the payroll, stated that the accounting was ready to be filed as of July 31, 2010 but that they were waiting for the transition of of the assets so that they could show a zero out balance, and that she would talk to her Client about staying on. The matter was continued to October 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the 5th Account and for proof of bond.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Vera Sprinkel. Charles Patterson for and with Dennis "Denny" Jaeger (Son of the Trustor). William Holsman for Emily Jaeger (Beneficiary and Settlor).

Ms. Ng stated that based upon the Examiner's notes she received yesterday that she amended the proposed order, that is not called this her client's final account, that she added a space for Mr. Jaeger's first report and account and a space for the bond, and that she had taken her client's fees off of the order because she is going to be filing another accounting. Mr. Holsman stated he is in favor of having Denny appointed, that he spoke to his client, that she is happy in Denny's home, and that there is a redemption clause in place to reduce Denny's share at the time of his Mother's death regarding the promissory note that he is currently paying. Mr. Patterson stated that his client is present, is willing to take over, and can get assistance when it's necessary to file the accounting. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the revised order), set Denny's bond at $429,000.00, set a status hearing on September 8, 2010 for the filing of Ms. Sprinkel's final account which will be from February 2010 until the assets are transferred to Denny, and set a status hearing September 7, 2011 for the filing of Denny's first account.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-17-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Vera Sprinkle. William Holsman for Emily Jaeger. Ronald Jaeger appeared by telephone.

All Parties stipulated to the Commissioner hearing the matter. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court approved the 2nd Account and Report, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Wong.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-29-2008 Continued To Sep-17-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Holsman for Emily Jaeger. Lillian Ng for Vera Sprinkel. Michael Jaeger and Ron Jaeger appeared by telephone.

Ms. Ng stated that she had filed her response to the objections and addressed the fee request. In addition, she stated that her client was helping the Settlor with assets outside of the trust and that her client was entitled to payment. Ron Jaeger raised issues regarding the $185,000.00 promissary note, outstanding loans/missing promissary notes to Denny, excessive attorney's fees and stated that his Mother was not capable of making business decisions. In addition, he questioned the value of the estate. The Court approved fees for services provided for the trust only. The fees have to be revised and the accounting corrections have to be reviewed. The matter was continued to September 17, 2008 at 10:30 a.m.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.
